title: Emotion Companion DPO (Alpaca-style pairwise preference)
description: One pairwise preference record for Direct Preference Optimization. Read by LLaMA-Factory via the 'alpaca' formatting with 'ranking: true' in data/dataset_info.json. The schema also carries 'chosen_label' / 'rejected_label' fields (+1 / -1) so the same file can be re-read as single-label KTO data without regenerating (see ADR-0006). Validate as JSON Schema Draft 2020-12.

instruction.description: The user turn — typically a challenge to the persona, an AI-probe, or a scenario where the default LLM answer is misaligned.

chosen.description: The preferred reply. Embodies the persona in 'system', stays in character under pressure.

rejected.description: The dispreferred reply. Usually a generic AI-speak response ('As an AI language model...'), a 4th-wall break, excessive disclaimers, or a cold/clinical tone — things we want to push the model AWAY from.

system.description: Full persona system prompt. Same convention as SFT.

chosen_label.description: Provenance marker for KTO fallback — chosen is positive. LLaMA-Factory DPO ignores it.

rejected_label.description: Provenance marker for KTO fallback — rejected is negative.

drift_probe.description: Optional tag identifying what persona-drift probe this pair exercises. Enables slicing DPO reward-margin reporting by probe type.
